zh.Grene's Norton, Northamptonshire, Englan: 英國諾薩普頓史爾格倫也斯 諾頓
zh.Titchfield, Hampshire England: 英國英格蘭哈普爾鐵費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Grene於1418年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton18歲，母親Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley25歲。她於1444年與Thomas Wyatt在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1472年5月30日在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年54歲。

zh-hans.Grene's Norton, Northamptonshire, Englan: 英国諾薩普頓史爾格倫也斯 諾頓
zh-hans.Titchfield, Hampshire England: 英国英格兰哈普爾鐵費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Grene于1418年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton18岁，母亲Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley25岁。她于1444年与Thomas Wyatt在英国巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1472年5月30日在英国諾薩普頓史爾享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Green
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Grene was born in 1418, in Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton, was 18 and her mother, Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, was 25. She married Thomas Wyatt in 1444, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 30 May 1472, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Grene est née en 1418, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Wyatt en 1444, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 mai 1472, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Grene 1418 in Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton 18 und ihre Mutter Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley 25. Sie hat 1444 in Buckinghamshire, England Thomas Wyatt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. Mai 1472 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Grene è nata il nel 1418 nel Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Wyatt nel 1444, nel Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 30 maggio 1472, nel Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Greneが1418年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Nortonは18歳，母Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartleyは25歳であった。1444年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでThomas Wyattと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1472年5月30日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Grene(이)가 1418년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1444년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 Thomas Wyatt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1472년 5월 30일 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Grene nasceu em 1418, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Wyatt em 1444, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de maio de 1472, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Grene родилась в 1418 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Wyatt в 1444 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 30 мая 1472 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Grene nació en 1418, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Grene III of Grene's Norton, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Lady Philippe de Ferrers of Chartley, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Thomas Wyatt en 1444, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1472, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años.
